name: Scallop and Green Bean Terrine
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon White pepper or to taste
1/2 pound Green beans, trimmed & cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor with a metal blade, puree the scallops with the egg white, salt, white pepper, and nutmeg. Transfer the puree to a metal bowl and chill, covered, for 1 hour.
2. Blanch the green beans in boiling salted water until tender, about 6 minutes. Drain and toss with butter, season with salt and white pepper, then reserve.
3. Set the bowl of puree in a larger bowl of ice water. Beat in 1 cup of creme fraiche at a time, until incorporated and fluffy. Fold in the green beans and spoon the mousse into a buttered 1-quart terrine.
4. Place the terrine in a baking pan and add enough hot water to reach two-thirds up the side of the terrine. Bake, covered with a buttered sheet of wax paper and the lid or double layer of foil, in a preheated 375°F oven for 45 minutes.
5. Remove the terrine from the pan and set aside. Let cool for at least 30 minutes before slicing.
6. Arrange slices in a buttered gratin dish large enough to hold them in one layer. Spread remaining butter lightly over each slice and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
7. Bake until hot and puffed slightly, about 20-25 minutes. Combine tomato coulis and remaining creme fraiche in a saucepan. Heat over moderate heat, stirring, until heated through. Serve alongside the terrine.
